name: Glen Campbell
abstract: Glen Travis Campbell (April 22, 1936 – August 8, 2017) was an American country musician and actor. He was best known for a series of hit songs in the 1960s and 1970s, and for hosting The Glen Campbell Goodtime Hour on CBS television from 1969 until 1972.

works.t: By the Time I Get to Phoenix

concerts.label: Glen Campbell: The Goodbye Tour
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I just played them with all these wonderful, again sort of 60s Wrecking Crew musicians. ... and Glen Campbell on rhythm guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: We just got through working the AT&T Golf Tournament up there with Clint Eastwood... and we had a great show. Diana Krall and Glen Campbell and Michael Bolton, and Clint Eastwood and his wife and my band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: And then all the variety shows started, the Carol Burnett, the Glen Campbell the — oh hell — there were about a dozen variety shows, and they always used big bands.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: back in those days there were a lot of TV shows that had live bands: Sonny & Cher, Glen Campbell, Carol Burnett come to mind.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: I was there where I played Elvis Presley, Ann-Margaret, Connie Stevens, Glen Campbell, the Osmonds, Liberace...
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: I grew up with the Beatles, Motown, Glen Campbell, Willie Nelson, the Temptations, Rolling Stones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019
